What we help with › Back pain
For all • All ages • Every stage of life

Back pain

Back pain is one of the most common reasons people come to see us, and one of the most treatable. Whether it came on suddenly, has crept up over months, or keeps returning, we help people of every age and activity level get comfortable, confident and moving again.

Understanding it · unique copy per page: what it feels like, why it happens in plain terms, reassurance · MUST include the areas-and-causes passage naming the joints, tissues (muscle, fascia, tendon, ligament) and causes (sport, accident, trauma, fall, gradual onset, underlying condition) relevant to this theme; this is the SEO body-coverage layer · sidebar stays verbatim
Understanding it

Why backs hurt, in plain terms

Back pain can show up as a dull ache after sitting, a sharp catch when you bend or lift, stiffness first thing in the morning, or pain that spreads into the buttock or leg. It is rarely a sign of serious damage. Far more often the muscles, joints and nerves of the back have become sensitised, guarded or overloaded, and with the right help that settles.

We see the whole spectrum here: lower back pain and lumbago, mid-back and thoracic discomfort, sciatica and nerve-related leg pain, and problems involving the muscles, fascia, tendons, ligaments, discs and joints of the spine and pelvis. It may have started with sport, lifting, an accident or a fall, long hours at a desk or the wheel, pregnancy, or simply crept in gradually with no obvious cause. Whatever the story, we assess carefully, explain what we find in plain language, and shape care around you rather than around a label.

Get in touch

The easiest first step is a conversation. Tell us what is going on and we will point you the right way, with no pressure at all.

Call 07773 814488
Three journey tiles · reframed from service benefits to the visitor's journey · no adjective rotation on condition pages · carries class "expect-navy" as the rhythm-break fallback because condition pages build copy-first; when the veil photo is chosen, remove "expect-navy", uncomment the veil section below and append its per-page CSS rule at the marked slot (180px canon, both simulations)
What to expect

From first call to feeling better

What is likely going on

Most back pain comes from sensitised or overworked muscles and joints rather than damage. Understanding that is often the first step to feeling better.

How we assess it

Your first visit is a conversation and a careful look at how you move, not just where it hurts. We take time to understand the whole picture before anything else.

What getting better looks like

A clear plan, hands-on care where it helps, and simple things you can do yourself. Progress at your pace, reviewed and adjusted as you improve.

Veiled photo band · 70% navy wash, homepage CTA treatment, at most once per page · requires this page's photo appended as the .veil CSS rule (see marked slot in the stylesheet) · run the photo overlay simulation before sign-off What it means for you

Back to the things you have been putting off

Gardening without paying for it the next day, lifting the grandchildren, getting back to the gym, or simply sitting through an evening in comfort. Whatever back pain has been taking from you, the aim is straightforward: to give it back.

The right care for this · NEW condition-page element, the page's routing job per the IA · promote the one or two services this condition routes to, benefit-led, with direct links · keep to two cards maximum
The right care for this

Where this usually leads

Pain & Injury Support

The usual starting point for back pain: careful assessment, hands-on treatment and a plan to settle things down and keep them settled.

Explore Pain & Injury Support ›

Clinical Remedial Massage

When tight, guarded muscles are a big part of the picture, focused hands-on work eases them and helps everything else move more freely.

Explore Clinical Remedial Massage ›
Reassurance · approved copy, reused verbatim from Our Approach · safe, gentle, at your pace Reassurance

Safe, gentle, and at your pace

Everything is done with care and at a pace that suits you, and nothing is rushed or forced. The aim throughout is simple: that you feel in good hands, understood, and steadily better.

Recognised and accountable

Held to recognised professional standards

Members and registrants of the Royal Society of Medicine, the Complementary and Natural Healthcare Council, the Institute for Functional Medicine, the Society of Sports Therapists, the Institute of Sport and Remedial Massage and the British Medical Acupuncture Society, among others.
